attributes.stem:
You are a medical student on your anaesthetics rotation, currently reviewing the following five pre-operative patients in holding bay before they go to their separate theatres.

Joel, a 27-year-old man undergoing a tonsillectomy. He is an occasional smoker, a social binge drinker and has a history of congenital renal agenesis, for which he currently requires no active management.

Jenny, a 23-year-old woman undergoing laparoscopic appendicectomy. She is a non-smoker, on no regular medications and is otherwise fit and well.

Mark, an 88-year-old man undergoing open inguinal hernia repair. He has a history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and is a current smoker who is compliant with his regular puffers. He doesn't drink and is independent from home.

Lynette, a 64-year-old woman undergoing a total laparoscopic hysterectomy. She is a current smoker, never drinks alcohol, and reports a history of hypertension and early onset Alzheimer's dementia. She lives at home with her husband who is her carer.

Diane, a 41-year-old woman undergoing washout and debridement of a dog bite on her forearm. She is a non-smoker, drinks alcohol socially, and has a history of hypercholesterolaemia and diet-controlled type II diabetes.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the patients reviewed is at the highest predicted risk of post-operative nausea and vomiting?
attributes.explanation:
Post-operative nausea and vomiting (PONV) is a common complication following surgery and anaesthesia. Several risk factors have been identified that increase the likelihood of PONV. These include female gender, non-smoking status, a history of motion sickness or previous PONV, and the use of postoperative opioids.

Several operations are also known to carry a higher risk of PONV - this is especially true for gastrointestinal surgery, gynaecological procedures, laparoscopic surgery, major orthopaedic surgery and procedures to the eyes or ears. Inhalational anaesthetics and the use of opioids also carry a higher risk compared to an intravenous or regional anaesthetic.

Therefore, Jenny is at the highest risk of PONV because she is a young woman, a non-smoker, and undergoing laparoscopic surgery, all of which are associated with a higher incidence of PONV.

Joel has a lower risk of PONV due to his male gender and occasional smoking status, which is protective against PONV.

Mark is also at a lower risk due to his male gender and current smoking status, despite his advanced age and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

Lynette has some risk factors for PONV, such as being female and undergoing laparoscopic surgery, but her smoking status would reduce her predicted risk compared to Jenny.

Diane has a lower risk of PONV due to the nature of her procedure and the absence of other significant risk factors, despite being female and a non-smoker.

If a patient is assessed to be at high risk of PONV, the anaesthetic approach can be tailored to try and reduce this possibility. This may involve avoiding inhalational agents and the administration of multimodal antiemetics intraoperatively (eg ondansetron, dexamethasone, droperidol).
learningPoints: Risk factors for post-operative nausea and vomiting (PONV) include female gender, non-smoking status, history of motion sickness or PONV, and use of postoperative opioids.
references: Jin Z, Gan TJ, Bergese SD. Prevention and Treatment of Postoperative Nausea and Vomiting (PONV): A Review of Current Recommendations and Emerging Therapies. Ther Clin Risk Manag. 2020 Dec 31;16:1305-1317. doi: 10.2147/TCRM.S256234. PMID: 33408475; PMCID: PMC7780848.

attributes.name: Graduating
attributes.description: Only suitable for a graduating medical student at the end of their degree
